refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve and maintain operational availability of ≥0.97 (97%) over the customer-defined evaluation period, measured as (total scheduled operating time − unplanned downtime) / total scheduled operating time.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-004 is a key performance indicator (KPI) that directly impacts mission success and stakeholder confidence. Refinement adds measurability (≥0.97 target), clarity on evaluation period, and explicit definition of availability metric to eliminate ambiguity between availability, reliability, and maintainability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous telemetry collection of operational state transitions (running/idle/failed/maintenance) across all NGHE solution components; statistical analysis of downtime events over agreed evaluation window; comparison against 0.97 threshold with confidence interval reporting.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and report all unplanned downtime events (failures, safety stops, communication loss) within 5 seconds.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall minimize mean time to repair (MTTR) through predictive maintenance and rapid fault isolation.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall provide redundancy or failover mechanisms for critical subsystems to reduce single-point-of-failure impact on availability.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain operational readiness during planned maintenance windows without impacting availability metric.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement continuous health monitoring and automated fault detection across all subsystems with real-time alerting to operators and maintenance teams.
DP-B: Design redundant critical subsystems (dual communication paths, backup power, secondary control channels) with automatic switchover logic.
DP-C: Establish predictive maintenance program using telemetry analytics to schedule maintenance during planned windows, minimizing unplanned downtime.
DP-D: Integrate all three approaches (monitoring + redundancy + predictive maintenance) into a unified availability management system with coordinated decision logic.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Integrate continuous health monitoring, redundant critical subsystems, and predictive maintenance into a unified availability management system with coordinated decision logic and real-time visibility across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services domains.

potential_sources: Health monitoring (DP-A) depends on telemetry interface availability; redundancy logic (DP-B) must coordinate with safety services to avoid conflicting failover decisions; predictive maintenance (DP-C) requires historical data aggregation across multiple subsystems.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is manageable through explicit interface contracts (Telemetry, SafetyAlert) and centralized decision logic. No circular dependencies detected. DP-D is decoupled from specific vehicle architecture, allowing flexibility in implementation.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Telemetry data is available continuously with <5s latency; Risk: Network Link failures could blind monitoring system.
Assumption: Redundant subsystems can be activated without operator intervention; Risk: Automatic failover logic errors could introduce new failure modes.
Assumption: Predictive maintenance models are accurate for customer's operational profile; Risk: Model drift over time or in new operational contexts could reduce effectiveness.

Assumption: Safety Services can coordinate with availability management without conflicting objectives; Risk: Safety-driven shutdowns may conflict with availability targets if not properly prioritized.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify priority hierarchy: Safety > Availability > Efficiency. Revise DP-D to explicitly state that safety-driven shutdowns are excluded from availability downtime calculations, and that predictive maintenance must be validated against customer's specific operational profile before deployment.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level availability management orchestrator that integrates health monitoring, redundancy coordination, and predictive maintenance across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Owns the unified decision logic, telemetry aggregation, and availability metric calculation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives continuous operational state, fault codes, and performance metrics from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems; required for health monitoring and predictive analytics.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety-critical alerts from Safety Services; sends availability-relevant events (e.g., predictive maintenance recommendations) that may impact safety posture.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends maintenance scheduling and failover activation commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems based on availability optimization logic.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Depends on Network Link for telemetry ingestion and command delivery; availability of Network Link directly impacts availability management effectiveness.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highest containment: NGHE Solution is the system-level orchestrator and natural owner of cross-subsystem availability logic. Minimal diffusion risk; all coordination logic centralized. Interfaces are well-defined and external.

Low containment: Vehicle is a subsystem, not a system-level orchestrator. Availability management logic would be scattered across vehicle health monitoring, redundancy, and maintenance scheduling, creating diffusion and coordination complexity.

Low containment: Teleoperation is a subsystem focused on remote operation. Availability management for the entire NGHE solution cannot be owned by a single subsystem; would require Teleoperation to have inappropriate visibility and control over Vehicle and Safety Services.

Low containment: Safety Services is a specialized subsystem for safety-critical functions. Availability management is a distinct concern; conflating them risks safety logic being compromised by availability optimization decisions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because: (1) Availability is a system-level KPI that requires cross-subsystem visibility and coordination; (2) Health monitoring, redundancy, and predictive maintenance are system-level concerns, not subsystem-specific; (3) Interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) are all external to NGHE Solution, enabling clean separation of concerns; (4) Ownership at NGHE Solution level avoids diffusion of availability logic across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution availability management depends on Telemetry interface; if Telemetry is unreliable or latency-prone, availability monitoring itself becomes unavailable.
Risk: Coordination between NGHE Solution and Safety Services via SafetyAlert could introduce race conditions or conflicting decisions if not carefully synchronized.

Risk: Predictive maintenance logic in NGHE Solution requires deep knowledge of Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystem failure modes; if models are inaccurate, false positives could trigger unnecessary maintenance, reducing availability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish explicit SLAs for Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces to ensure NGHE Solution availability management is not bottlenecked by subsystem communication. Define clear priority rules for SafetyAlert conflicts (e.g., safety-driven shutdowns always take precedence). Implement model validation and continuous retraining for predictive maintenance to adapt to customer operational profiles.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (unified availability management system) directly addresses FR-NGHE-004 (achieve ≥0.97 availability). DP-D is necessary and sufficient: it provides continuous monitoring (detects downtime), redundancy (reduces impact), and predictive maintenance (minimizes unplanned downtime). No independence violations; DP-D is a single, coherent solution concept.

diffusion_risks: Moderate risk: If availability management logic is split between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation subsystems, coordination complexity increases and diffusion of responsibility could lead to gaps in monitoring or conflicting failover decisions.
Low risk: Interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) are well-defined and external; NGHE Solution can own the orchestration logic without intrusive coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution containment score of 0.95 is strong. Diffusion score of 0.05 indicates minimal risk of logic scattering. Ownership is clear and unambiguous. Recommended to proceed with NGHE Solution as IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High: Availability metric (≥0.97) is measurable via telemetry-based downtime tracking. NGHE Solution can aggregate data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to calculate system-level availability. Verification is feasible during integration testing and field deployment.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear: FR-NGHE-004 is allocated to NGHE Solution as the system-level orchestrator. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through 004.4) are allocated to specific subsystems (Vehicle for fault detection, Teleoperation for communication redundancy, Safety Services for safety-driven shutdowns) with NGHE Solution coordinating across them.

recommended_revisions: Establish explicit SLAs for Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces to ensure NGHE Solution availability management is not bottlenecked by subsystem communication latency or reliability.
Define clear priority rules for SafetyAlert conflicts: safety-driven shutdowns always take precedence over availability optimization.
Implement model validation and continuous retraining for predictive maintenance to adapt to customer operational profiles and reduce false positives.
Add traceability matrix linking FR-NGHE-004 and sub-FRs to specific Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystem requirements to ensure no gaps in allocation.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-004 is a system-level KPI that is not atomic; it requires decomposition into sub-FRs to drive detailed design. The refined FR and DP are sound, and IM assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate. However, the four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-004.1 through 004.4) must be decomposed further to allocate specific design responsibilities to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to ensure complete coverage and avoid ambiguity in subsystem design.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-004.1: The NGHE solution shall detect and report all unplanned downtime events (failures, safety stops, communication loss) within 5 seconds.
FR-NGHE-004.2: The NGHE solution shall minimize mean time to repair (MTTR) through predictive maintenance and rapid fault isolation.
FR-NGHE-004.3: The NGHE solution shall provide redundancy or failover mechanisms for critical subsystems to reduce single-point-of-failure impact on availability.
FR-NGHE-004.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain operational readiness during planned maintenance windows without impacting availability metric.

assumptions: Telemetry data is available continuously with <5s latency from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems.
Redundant subsystems can be activated without operator intervention via automated failover logic.
Predictive maintenance models are accurate for customer's operational profile and can be validated before deployment.
Safety Services can coordinate with availability management without conflicting objectives; safety-driven shutdowns are excluded from availability downtime calculations.
Network Link is sufficiently reliable to support continuous telemetry ingestion and command delivery.
Availability target of ≥0.97 is achievable with current technology and operational practices; customer has validated this target against their business requirements.
open_risks: Network Link failures could blind NGHE Solution availability monitoring, creating a single point of failure for the availability management system itself.
Automatic failover logic errors could introduce new failure modes or safety hazards if not rigorously validated.
Predictive maintenance model drift over time or in new operational contexts could reduce effectiveness and increase false positives.
Coordination between NGHE Solution and Safety Services via SafetyAlert could introduce race conditions or conflicting decisions if not carefully synchronized.
Customer's operational profile may change over time, requiring continuous retraining of predictive maintenance models.
evidence_needed: Telemetry interface SLA specification (latency, availability, data completeness) from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems.
SafetyAlert interface specification and priority rules for conflict resolution between safety and availability objectives.
Predictive maintenance model validation results against customer's historical operational data.
Redundancy and failover logic design documentation with failure mode analysis (FMEA) to identify and mitigate new failure modes.
Network Link reliability analysis and contingency plans for communication loss scenarios.
Customer acceptance criteria for availability metric calculation methodology and evaluation period.
